title: Adina spathellifera
intro: Adina spathellifera, also known as the Kola tree or Red cola, is a tropical plant species native to West Africa. The bark and leaves of the Adina spathellifera have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, and it is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Adina spathellifera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Adina spathellifera is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or made into a tea, to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Adina spathellifera is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to tropical Asia, specifically in countries such as India, Sri Lanka, and Southeast Asia. The plant is a shrub or small tree that grows up to 10 meters tall, with elliptical leaves and small white flowers. Adina spathellifera is a member of the Rubiaceae family, which includes over 600 genera and 13,000 species of flowering plants. This family is one of the largest and most diverse in the world, with members found in a wide range of habitats, from tropical rainforests to dry scrublands.
